📊 Solana continues to lead the pack.

With $1.14B in 24H DEX volume, @Solana remains the #1 chain, staying ahead of every other ecosystem.

Another notable name on the leaderboard: @RobinhoodCrypto Chain has already climbed to #3 with $808M in 24H DEX volume — an early sign that tokenized assets are becoming an increasingly important part of on-chain activity.

The competition is evolving, but Solana is still setting the pace. ⚡

For any team launching an asset, the fastest way to a live market is to land where the traders, the liquidity, and the routing already are.

Learn more about how @sunrise works:
 https://t.co/8PNEk32E3X

Tokenized equities are landing the same way. SpaceX's SPCX went live on Solana the same day it IPO'd on Nasdaq, issued through @Backpack Securities.

It did over $50 million in the first 24 hours across 51 markets, and tracked the Nasdaq price almost to the cent: $172.82 here versus $172.86 there.

In six months, new assets that launched on Solana traded $3.5B across 14M trades and 221,000 wallets.

Most of them were born on other chains (like AVAX, HYPE, UNI or MON).

Now some of their deepest trading happens right here via @sunrise 🧵 https://t.co/6hVxT7aKMX

RT @Luketruitt: Credit order books are the future 🚀🚀

For the first time in a long time I don't feel anxious about the idea of buying and holding crypto for the longterm

With this I don't mean it can't go down anymore, but that the price action that follows will not be news driven, it will be the normal course of action of an asset forming a bottom over months

> Saylor FUD is over
> 4 year cycle bottom plays out in the next 2-3 months, we are closing in on a time to buy vs to sell/ignore
> DATs that wanted to sell would've sold by now, we've probably priced sells that haven't even happened yet

What comes next is a boring chop on a big range of maybe $50K to $70K, and then a slow recovery into a new bull market.

If I buy at $64K or $60K I probably am going to be underwater for some time but at this point all I care about is time, not price

In late bear market stages you shouldn't care as much about price because the time when you buy is more important

You can't guess the bottom, let alone buy it

But you can look at where we are in the cycle and conclude that any buys in the next XYZ weeks/months will age well in 2 years

That's the gameplan for me now

The market I picked: 👇

https://t.co/NBfQFbDAHT

Tomorrow is a huge day for Solana.

@jtx_trade opens to everyone.

JTX: "The way you trade on Solana changes on Tuesday. We are going to make every other trading product on Solana feel small. We are going to make every CEX in the world feel slow. Give us a few months. You'll see."

→ 80% of all fees flow back to $JTO. 20% reinvested into the product
- Refer a friend, earn 20% of their fees. Forever
- Batch auction mechanism in development

→ Roadmap:
- Spot trading first → perps (Phoenix integration) → prediction markets
- Spot routes across every DEX on Solana. 
- Self custody by default. Your keys never leave your hands
- Pro order types: resting limits, brackets, OCO, stop orders, TWAP, plus TradingView charts baked in

→ BAM: JTX runs on Jito's BAM plugins, sequencing transactions in secure hardware (TEEs) with proposer builder separation on Solana. Encrypted until execution: no frontrunning, no sandwiches, provable ordering.

→ FullSend (Jito x Privy, owned by Stripe): routes every transaction to the current and upcoming block leaders. ~200ms down to ~50ms inclusion, near 99.999% landing.

→ @dflow: Solana's only JIT routing aggregator. Reroutes at execution for minimal slippage and filters toxic flow, so you trade against deep liquidity, not the operator.

→ Receipts: every trade shows exactly what you saved vs a CEX, verifiable onchain. CEX is the floor. JTX is the ceiling.

→ JTX hinted it themselves: refer users with your reflink and you may land Day 1 access on the 14th. That window closes today.
https://t.co/CCwtl8dwUw

pakai Claude Code tiap hari tapi sering ngerasa hasilnya kurang konsisten kadang bagus, kadang berantakan?  

masalahnya bukan di model-nya. biasanya di struktur workflow-nya.  

AI Auto-Work adalah sistem yang ngatur alur kerja AI coding dari awal sampai akhir bukan cuma kasih prompt dan berharap hasilnya bagus.  

alurnya: requirement → research & planning → task decomposition → development → review → testing → commit. setiap tahap pakai file terpisah, jadi context gak numpuk jadi satu yang panjang dan berantakan.

yang paling menarik: dia pakai dual-model loop. Claude Code sebagai coder, Codex sebagai reviewer. jadi setiap kode yang digenerate langsung direview otomatis sebelum lanjut ke tahap berikutnya.  

manfaat yang paling kerasa: 
→ context lebih bersih karena terisolasi per tahap 
→ ada quality gates otomatis (compile, unit test, smoke test) sebelum masuk review 
→ ada fast path mode untuk perubahan kecil biar gak harus lewat semua tahap 
→ commit otomatis dengan pesan yang proper  

cocok buat kalian yang udah serius pakai Claude Code dan mau hasilnya lebih predictable. masih relatif baru dan butuh setup awal, tapi konsepnya solid.  

kalian biasanya handle workflow AI coding kalian gimana sekarang?  

github: https://t.co/TDf7k5zo52

Thank you for showing up 🙏

We asked you for feedback on Jupiter Spot Early Preview

So many of you tried it out and shared your feedback

And we've read every single piece.

Round 1 is closed, and the team is already building your feedback in.

Once those changes are live, we'll open Round 2 of feedback.

COMING SOON

Jupiter hasn't stopped shipping all month, here are the latest updates from last week

- Earn on Recurring.
Funds in your DCA orders no longer sit idle. They earn yield in Jupiter Lend until each buy fills.

- USDC Rewards for Good Feedback
Drop some feedback on jupiter spot, what you'd want, what's missing, what you'd hate to lose. The top 10 most useful pieces of feedback get rewarded in USDC

- Jupiter VIP
If you’re in a VIP program elsewhere, Jupiter gives you a flat 3% cashback on your Jupiter card once you’re verified and approved.

- Jupiter Q2 ASR
You can claim your Active Staking Rewards from the jupiter rewards tab until October 8th

di 2026, hampir semua developer yang serius coding udah pakai minimal satu AI coding tool. tapi mana yang worth dipake?  

gua breakdown tiga yang paling sering dibahas:  

Claude Code 
terminal-native agent yang bisa kerja otonom bikin plan sendiri, edit multi-file, fix error, iterate sampai selesai. paling kuat untuk task kompleks dan codebase besar. kelemahannya: tidak ada visual editor, dan hanya bisa pakai model Claude. 
→ ~$20/bulan (Claude Pro)  

Cursor 
AI IDE berbasis VS Code. tab completion pintar, visual diff, composer mode untuk multi-file editing. paling enak untuk workflow coding harian karena interfacenya familiar. 
→ free tier terbatas, Pro ~$20/bulan  

OpenCode
open-source terminal agent yang bisa pakai 75+ provider Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, bahkan local model via Ollama. paling fleksibel dan paling hemat. 160K+ GitHub stars. 
→ gratis (bayar API-nya aja, atau pakai local) 

yang menarik: 70%+ developer sekarang pakai kombinasi, bukan pilih satu. pattern yang paling umum Cursor untuk editing harian, Claude Code untuk refactoring besar, OpenCode untuk eksperimen atau hemat biaya.  

rekomendasi simpel: 
→ suka visual editor → mulai dari Cursor 
→ sering kerja di codebase besar → Claude Code 
→ mau gratis dan fleksibel → OpenCode

Core claim: multi-chain trading, DAO governance, staking rewards, and revenue sharing were marketed as real utility, then delayed, changed, or never fully delivered
What was your experience with the Magic Eden marketplace and token? 🤔

3. June 2022: $130M Series B, $1.6B valuation

Full year 2022 revenue: $96 M

Broader NFT market implodes - daily volume across all marketplaces goes from $17B in January 2022 to $500M by September, a 97% collapse

Magic Eden goes multi-chain anyway, chasing Ethereum and Bitcoin

2. Launchpad is minting daily, NFTs become the biggest meta

Then the rugs hit.

February 2022: Balloonsville rugs ~5,000 SOL (~$600K), then publicly taunts Magic Eden for never enforcing the KYC/doxxing it claimed to require. 

Degen Town follows mid-2022 - another biggest mess with people losing money. 

ME's response: refund some people, promise stricter vetting

1. Magic Eden launches September 2021 as Solana NFT marketplace

Low fees, clean UX, a Launchpad everyone wants in on. By April 2022 it's doing $400M in monthly GMV, $8M in monthly revenue.

$1B cumulative volume in 8 months, 90%+ of Solana NFT market share

Magic Eden: NFT dominance and hype turned into a 99% token crash and lawsuit

10 Reasons why $ANSEM could still go to $1B:

1 | Skin in the game:
Unlike most KOL coins, creator fees (already over $1.4M generated) and airdrops flow back to holders and the trenches.

2 | 1M+ Followers:
With 1M+ followers, every Ansem post, reply about “Black Bull” triggers massive organic posts across CT. Attention is the most valuable currency.

3 | $WIF Blueprint:
Ansem (@blknoiz06) single-handedly helped turn $WIF into a multi-billion Solana success story. Why not doing it a second time?

4 | Explosive Holder Growth:
Already 129K+ holders which are an organic marketing army that scales virality.

5 | Path to Major Listings:
As MC grows toward $1B, natural CEX listings (Binance, Coinbase, etc.) follow

6 | Powerful “Black Bull” Narrative:
A perfect narrative at a boring time. One that literally grabs everyone’s attention.

7 | General Support:
Ansem gets support not only from his community, but also from Solana and other big KOLs, chains, and companies—basically everyone rooting for a success story.

8 | Strong onchain Data:
After just a few weeks, $ANSEM not only has deeper liquidity than most OG memecoins, but also leads in volume day after day.

9 | Bull Market as Catalyst:
We're still in a bear market. But what happens if BTC goes back to $70-80K??

10 | Risk-Reward Asymmetry:
From ~$100M MC to $1B is a realistic 8-10x in a strong run (many memes have done 50-100x). Low relative to top memes’ peaks, with proven resilience through corrections, makes it attractive for new capital.
